items.title: Apple says it may remove some apps from the App Store if they don’t attract users
items.content: Apple may begin removing existing apps that it considers stale, low-value, or unable to attract users.

items.title: iOS 27 features we didn’t see onstage
items.content: While iOS 27 isn't bringing major changes, it's still got many small features that could improve everyday usage for iPhone users.

items.title: Apple brings streaming-style subscription bundles to the App Store
items.content: Apple is expanding its App Bundles feature to allow developers to partner with one another on discounted subscription packages.

items.title: Apple’s App Store rolls out personalized recommendations
items.content: Apple will now recommend apps based on your downloads and behavior.

items.title: Microsoft’s open source tools were hacked to steal passwords of AI developers
items.content: Microsoft shut down dozens of GitHub code repositories for Azure and AI coding tools after a reported hack.

items.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
items.content: We're likely to see more price increases as the big AI companies plan to go public.

items.title: Reid Hoffman is leaving Microsoft’s board to go ‘founder mode’ with  startup Manas
items.content: After a very profitable decade on Microsoft's board, Reid Hoffman is stepping down to focus on his AI drug discovery startup Manus.

items.title: New Microsoft tool lets devs spin up AI behavior tests using text descriptions
items.content: Microsoft on Tuesday took the wraps off Adaptive Spec-driven Scoring for Evaluation and Regression Testing, an open source framework for spinning up AI evaluations.

items.title: Billionaires’ Billions Are Increasing Faster Than Ever
items.content: Elon Musk’s potential new status as a trillionaire demonstrates in real time why there has been such a rapid rise in the concentration of wealth at the top.

items.title: Dutch Authorities Block $115 Million Deal by a U.S. Tech Company
items.content: The Netherlands blocked a U.S. company from buying a Dutch firm that handles its national ID system, saying it would create a “threat to the public interest.”

items.title: Why Apple’s A.I. Upgrade for Siri Won’t Be Available in Europe
items.content: A regulatory dispute has indefinitely delayed the release of Siri AI.

items.title: U.S. Exports Rose in April, as War with Iran Buoyed Oil Demand
items.content: Commerce Department data showed exports rose in April, slightly outpacing the growth in imports.
